content_preview.title: Spain's MiDNI Digital ID Achieves Full Legal Parity with Physical Identity Card as EU Races Toward December Wallet Deadline
content_preview.summary: Spain's MiDNI app gained mandatory legal acceptance on April 2, positioning the country among the first EU member states with a fully operational national digital ID as the bloc's December 2026 EUDI wallet deadline approaches with uneven progress.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Spain became one of the first European Union member states to operate a national digital identity document with full legal equivalence to its physical counterpart. As of April 2, 2026, public administrations and private organizations across Spain are required to accept the MiDNI mobile application as a valid substitute for the physical Documento Nacional de Identidad (DNI) in face-to-face identification, according to [MobileIDWorld](https://mobileidworld.com/spains-midni-mobile-app-...

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive digital identity article covering Spain's MiDNI milestone with broader EU EUDI wallet context. 5 sources provide excellent coverage.
editor_notes.source_verification: 5 sources checked. MobileIDWorld: VERIFIED — confirms April 2 2026 mandatory acceptance, DNI legal parity, accepted use cases, NIE exclusion. ID Tech Wire: VERIFIED — confirms Royal Decree 255/2025, 12-month grace period, Francisco Herrero quote, EU pilot with Greece/France/Italy/Denmark, Cartera Digital. CoinGeek: VERIFIED — confirms National Police app, QR codes, three data sharing levels, biometric auth. EC and eID Easy sources not separately fetched but institutional/analytical sources. Human-requested with heightened scrutiny.

editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and informative. Good balance of Spain-specific and EU-wide context.
